name: City Bank & Trust Co.
description_short: FDIC-insured community bank serving Nebraska since 1910 with six locations offering personal and business checking, savings, lending, and mobile banking services.
description_long:
City Bank & Trust Co. is a Nebraska-based community bank that has operated since 1910, making it over 110 years old. The bank is headquartered in Lincoln with additional locations in Crete, Seward, and an East Branch that was recently opened. As a Member FDIC institution, all deposits are backed by federal insurance and the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government, providing account protection up to standard FDIC limits.

The bank offers comprehensive banking services for both personal and business customers. On the personal side, they provide checking and savings accounts, money market accounts, CDs, IRAs, and Health Savings Accounts. Lending products include auto loans, personal loans, mortgage lending (home loans, construction loans, bridge loans, and home equity loans). Their digital services include online banking, bill pay, eStatements, mobile banking with mobile deposit capabilities, and integration with Zelle for peer-to-peer transfers. Business customers can access business checking, business savings, business money market accounts, merchant services, ACH and payroll services, positive pay/check reconciliation, and merchant card processing.

City Bank & Trust Co. distinguishes itself through its deep agricultural lending expertise, offering agriculture real estate loans, operating lines of credit, equipment loans, and livestock loans—services tailored to Nebraska's farming community. The bank has invested in modernizing its digital experience with a redesigned website optimized for mobile devices and a native mobile app available on both iOS and Android. They offer additional services like safe deposit boxes, overdraft protection, QuickBooks integration, and fraud protection tools. Their customer service is accessible via phone at (402) 826-2144, with multiple physical locations for in-person banking.

As a regional community bank, City Bank & Trust Co. is best suited for customers who value local relationships and personalized service, particularly those in Nebraska with agricultural or small business needs. However, customers seeking cutting-edge fintech features or extensive branch networks outside Nebraska should consider larger institutions. The bank's strength lies in traditional banking fundamentals and community commitment rather than innovative digital-first banking. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Mortgage lending including home loans, construction loans, and home equity loans
Business checking, savings, and money market accounts
Agricultural lending including livestock loans, farm equipment loans, and operating lines of credit
Commercial real estate loans and SBA loans
Online banking, bill pay, and eStatements
Mobile banking with mobile deposit and Zelle transfers
Merchant services and merchant card processing
pros: FDIC-insured deposits with full federal backing, protecting accounts up to standard limits
Specialized agricultural lending products including livestock loans and farm equipment financing
Modern mobile app available on iOS and Android with mobile deposit capability
Six physical locations across Nebraska (Lincoln, Crete, Seward, and East Branch) for local service
Comprehensive business services including merchant processing, ACH, payroll, and remote deposit capture
Over 110 years of continuous operation and community presence in Nebraska since 1910
Zelle integration for peer-to-peer money transfers included with accounts
cons: Limited geographic footprint with only six locations, all in Nebraska—not suitable for multi-state customers
No mention of competitive rates or rate guarantees on their website, requiring customers to call for pricing
Website content indicates recent login system changes and technical updates, suggesting potential ongoing infrastructure transitions
No evidence of premium accounts, wealth management services, or investment products beyond basic CDs and IRAs
Limited transparency on fees, minimum balance requirements, and account terms on the public website
best_for: Nebraska agricultural businesses and farmers requiring specialized farm lending and equipment financing
Small to medium-sized Nebraska businesses needing merchant services, payroll processing, and local banking relationships
Nebraska residents preferring community banking over national chains and valuing local decision-making
Customers seeking traditional full-service banking with mobile and online capabilities in the Lincoln/Crete/Seward area
diagnosis: City Bank & Trust Co. is best for Nebraska-based individuals and businesses seeking personalized community banking with strong agricultural lending expertise and local decision-making. The main caveat is limited geographic reach outside Nebraska and lack of transparency on rates and fees on their public website, requiring direct customer contact for specific pricing information.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
